extra.message: User input from request.args.get('search') is directly interpolated into an SQL query via f-string at line 137: f"SELECT filename FROM files WHERE user_id = {user_id} AND filename LIKE '%{search_query}%'". An attacker can inject arbitrary SQL via the search parameter, e.g., ' UNION SELECT password FROM users--.

The custom_hash function uses hashlib.md5() to hash passwords. MD5 is cryptographically broken and unsuitable for password hashing — it is fast to compute, making brute-force attacks trivial. A proper key derivation function like bcrypt, scrypt, or argon2 should be used instead.

The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as "just_another_secret" in source code. This key is used for session signing and JWT token generation. An attacker with access to the source can forge session cookies and JWT tokens, leading to complete authentication bypass.

DEBUG mode is set to True in the configuration. In production, this exposes the Werkzeug interactive debugger, which allows arbitrary code execution via the debug console, and leaks detailed error tracebacks to attackers.

cwe: CWE-215: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Debugging Code

extra.message: SESSION_COOKIE_HTTPONLY is set to False, which allows JavaScript running in the browser to access the session cookie via document.cookie. This makes the application vulnerable to session theft via XSS attacks.

The update_picture endpoint fetches content from an arbitrary URL provided by the user via requests.get(picture_url) at line 254. An attacker can supply internal URLs (e.g., http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/ for cloud metadata, or http://localhost:port/ for internal services) to probe and access internal resources. There is no URL validation or allowlist.

The /api/v1/delete-user/<user_id> endpoint has its @token_required decorator commented out (line 221-222), meaning no authentication is required. Any unauthenticated user can delete any account by sending a DELETE request with the target user_id.

The /api/v1/profile endpoint has its @token_required decorator commented out (line 152-153), meaning no authentication is required. Any unauthenticated user can query any user's profile (username, email, country, role, permissions, team) by providing the user_id query parameter. This also constitutes an IDOR vulnerability since any user can access any other user's data.

cwe: CWE-639: Authorization Bypass Through User-Controlled Key

extra.message: The /api/v1/update_picture endpoint has no authentication decorator at all. Any unauthenticated user can set any user's profile picture by providing a username and a URL, combining with the SSRF vulnerability to attack internal services and overwrite profile pictures.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: The upload_file route uses the raw filename from file.filename (line 262) directly in os.path.join(UPLOAD_FOLDER, filename) and file.save(filepath). An attacker can set the filename to ../../../../etc/cron.d/malicious to write files outside the intended upload directory, potentially achieving remote code execution or overwriting critical system files.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The delete_file route constructs a file path using os.path.join(UPLOAD_FOLDER, filename) where filename comes from the URL parameter. If a file was previously uploaded with a path traversal filename (e.g., ../../tmp/important), os.remove(file_path) on line 364 will delete the file at the traversed path, allowing deletion of arbitrary files on the server.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')

extra.message: The search_query variable is rendered with the | safe filter ({{ search_query | safe }}) which disables Jinja2's auto-escaping. Since search_query comes from request.args.get('search') (server/routes.py:126), an attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript via the search parameter, e.g., <script>alert(document.cookie)</script>, which will execute in the victim's browser.

The user.country field is rendered with the | safe filter ({{ user.country | safe }}) which disables Jinja2's auto-escaping. Since the country value is set by the user during signup or profile update, this is a stored XSS vulnerability — an attacker can set their country to <script>...</script> and the script will execute whenever any user views their profile.

jQuery's .append() method is used to insert user-controlled comment input directly as HTML: $commentList.append('
  • ' + comment + '
    • '). This allows DOM-based XSS — an attacker can type as a comment, which will be parsed and executed as HTML.

The exception handler returns str(e) in the response body at line 298: return {'message': str(e)}, 500. This leaks internal error details (file paths, database errors, network information) to the client, which can aid attackers in reconnaissance.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The update_profile route processes 'role' and 'permissions' from request.form even though these fields are hidden in the HTML form (edit_profile.html lines 39-43 are commented out). An attacker can craft a POST request with role=admin&permissions=all to escalate their privileges. Lines 187-196 show that if 'role' or 'permissions' are present in request.form, their values are used directly.

The upload_file route does not validate file type, extension, or content. Any file can be uploaded, including .html (for stored XSS), .py (for code execution if the upload directory is accessible), or .php/.jsp files on appropriate servers. There is no allowlist or denylist of file types.

cwe: CWE-434: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type

extra.message: The /uploads/profile_pictures/<filename> route has no authentication or authorization check. Any unauthenticated user can access any profile picture by guessing the numeric filename (e.g., 1.png, 2.png). Unlike other routes that check 'username' in session, this route simply serves any requested file from the profile pictures directory.
